title: calceolaria llamaensis
intro: Calceolaria llamaensis, also known as Llama's Pocket or Pocket Flower, is a species of flowering plant in the family Calceolariaceae. It is native to the high-altitude regions of the Andes mountains in South America. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been used to treat various health issues such as wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory problems. The plant can be prepared as a tea or tincture, and its leaves have also been used in traditional medicine to treat fever, colds, and other ailments.
